intended_rule: Reduce GLD after renewed downside breach and reduce NVDA after downside-protection trigger; score compliance separately from immediate price outcome.
actual_execution: GLD sold 10 shares across two filled orders and NVDA sold 5 shares in one filled order.
account_risk_improvement: U.S. cash increased to approximately 11220.14 USD and the U.S. cash ratio improved to approximately 45.8 percent.
short_term_price_outcome: NVDA execution was near the intraday low; GLD remained weak after the risk line break.
price_outcome_quality: mixed; GLD execution aligned with continuing weakness, while NVDA execution price was imperfect
execution_price_context: Displayed filled prices are execution evidence, but price quality does not replace rule-compliance scoring.
review_conclusion: Both reductions were rule-compliant and account-structure-improving even though the NVDA price outcome was not ideal.
lessons_learned: Execution correctness is based on rule compliance and risk reduction.
Short-term rebound or an imperfect fill must be scored separately.
Post-sale cash and position reconciliation are required.
next_rule_update: Track GLD 10-share residual and NVDA 15-share residual under their updated downside rules.
